message:
test(fcp-core): pin RiskTier + QuorumPurpose serde tag forms (flywheel_connectors-jtbfy)

Bead asks for MeshNodeRole serde tag. fcp-core has no type literally
named MeshNodeRole; the role-shaped classifiers that decide what a
mesh node is doing in a quorum operation split across RiskTier
(quorum.rs:56, 4 variants Safe/Risky/Dangerous/CriticalWrite) and
QuorumPurpose (quorum.rs:88, 8 variants AuditHead / ZoneCheckpoint
/ RevocationHead / DangerousLease / RiskyLease / SafeLease /
KeyRotation / MembershipChange). Neither carries
#[serde(rename_all = ...)] so the wire form is the PascalCase
variant name verbatim — DIFFERENT from RiskTier::as_str/Display
which return snake_case. Pin the dual encoding loudly because
operator tooling that filters audit logs MUST know which form to
expect from each channel.

Coverage:
- RiskTier::as_str snake_case tokens pinned per variant (the
  documented Display surface)
- RiskTier::Display byte-for-byte agrees with as_str
- RiskTier serde JSON form is PascalCase variant name (drift from
  rename_all swap surfaces here)
- Display vs serde DISAGREE intentionally on CriticalWrite —
  pinned as a sentinel for any future rename_all swap that would
  silently rebrand audit-log tokens
- RiskTier JSON + CBOR round-trip per variant; CBOR encodes as Text
- QuorumPurpose serde JSON form is PascalCase per variant
- QuorumPurpose JSON + CBOR round-trip per variant
- QuorumPurpose::default_risk_tier() truth table per variant —
  documented mapping pinned (AuditHead/ZoneCheckpoint/RevocationHead
  → CriticalWrite; DangerousLease/KeyRotation/MembershipChange →
  Dangerous; RiskyLease → Risky; SafeLease → Safe)
- Pairwise distinctness for both enums
- Lower snake_case rejected for both enums; multi-word variants
  exposed (critical_write / audit_head / zone_checkpoint /
  key_rotation / membership_change all rejected)
- Variant-count sentinels (4 RiskTier, 8 QuorumPurpose)
- Cross-format consistency: JSON and CBOR decode to the same
  variant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
